text:
corresponds to
Dasa in Sanskrit and to decem in Latin. Greek astrologers
attached great importance to this decanate possibly on account
of the special significance of the number ten. In India too we
associate this number with some mystic significance. Look at
the ten Avataras. ten quarters, ten heads of Ravana etc. The
Bible too speaks of the ten Commandments. This ten degree
portion of a Sign represents in fact a part of man's existence on
this earth. This might also account for the acceptance by ancient
scholars of man's span of life to be 120 years in all. The impor-
tance of the decanate may be gathered from the fact that great
scholars like Varahamihira have bestowed unusual thought on
this subject.
There is difference of opinion among the authorities regarding
the ownership of the decanates in the different Signs. Vara-
hamihira is of opinion that in all Signs the lords of decanates are
(i) the lord of the Sign itself, (ii) that of the fifth house therefrom,
